grooves.description: Kick on every beat with backbeat snare — the foundation of house and dance music. Quarter-note scoring grid.
grooves.name: Four on the Floor (1/4)

grooves.description: Kick on every beat with backbeat snare. Eighth-note scoring grid for tighter timing.
grooves.name: Four on the Floor (1/8)
Four on the Floor (1/16)
grooves.description: Kick on every beat with backbeat snare. Sixteenth-note scoring grid for the strictest timing.

grooves.name: Basic Rock (1/4)
grooves.description: Kick on 1 & 3, snare on 2 & 4, driving eighth-note hi-hats. Quarter-note scoring grid.

grooves.name: Basic Rock (1/8)
grooves.description: The essential rock beat: kick on 1 & 3, snare on 2 & 4, eighth-note hi-hats.

grooves.name: Basic Rock (1/16)
grooves.description: The essential rock beat with a sixteenth-note scoring grid for precise timing work.

grooves.name: Half-Time
grooves.description: Snare moved to beat 3 for a wide, spacious half-time feel.

grooves.name: Disco
grooves.description: Four-on-the-floor kick, backbeat snare and steady sixteenth-note hi-hats.

grooves.name: Boom Bap
grooves.description: Classic laid-back hip-hop backbeat with a kick on the 'and' of 3.

grooves.name: Behind the Beat (1/8)
grooves.description: Straight eighth-note rock pattern where every scoring quantum sits slightly behind the click — practice playing in the pocket.

grooves.name: Behind the Beat (1/16)
grooves.description: Same laid-back drag on a sixteenth-note grid.

grooves.name: Linn Shuffle (1/16)
grooves.description: The classic 58% MPC/Linn swing on sixteenths — that unmistakable hip-hop bounce.

grooves.name: Shuffle
grooves.description: Triplet-based swing feel — the off-beats land on the triplet.

grooves.name: Tresillo
grooves.description: The 3+3+2 Afro-Cuban rhythm at the heart of countless Latin and pop grooves.

grooves.name: Habanera
grooves.description: The dotted Cuban dance rhythm made famous by Bizet's Carmen.

grooves.name: Bossa Clave
grooves.description: The two-bar bossa nova clave pattern played on claves.

grooves.name: Bossa Nova
grooves.description: Cross-stick on the clave, syncopated kick and steady eighth-note hi-hats.

grooves.name: Reggae (One Drop)
grooves.description: Kick and snare together on beat 3 with off-beat skank — the one-drop feel.

grooves.name: Subdivision Drill
grooves.description: An accelerating drill: quarters, then eighths, then a run of sixteenths in one bar.

grooves.name: Straight to Swing
grooves.description: Beats 1–2 play straight eighths, beats 3–4 swing — drill for switching feels mid-bar.

grooves.name: Subdivision Pyramid
grooves.description: The note-value pyramid: a quarter, two eighths, an eighth-note triplet, then four sixteenths.

grooves.name: Half-Time Shuffle
grooves.description: Shuffled hi-hats with a half-time backbeat — the 'Rosanna' feel.

grooves.name: 3 Beat Variation #1
grooves.description: Eighth-note groove with backbeat snare, kicks on 1, 3, 3a, 4a, and open hi-hat on 4a.

grooves.name: 3 Beat Variation #2
grooves.description: Sixteenth-note groove with kicks on 1, 1a, 3 and soft hi-hats on 3e, 3a.

grooves.name: 3 Beat Variation #3
grooves.description: Sixteenth-note groove with ghost snare on 2&, kicks on 1, 3, 3&, 4, and open hi-hat on 4&.
